lecture_context.lecture_title: Stanford CS231N Lecture 2: Image Classification with Linear Classifiers

unit.name: Data-driven pipeline and nearest neighbor basics
unit.description: Contrasts hand-designed vision pipelines with a data-driven learning workflow, then builds the nearest neighbor classifier from its train and predict procedures.
unit.summary: This segment argues that hard-coded if-then logic is poorly suited to image understanding, noting older edge-and-corner pipelines were difficult to scale and required object-specific rules [ts=755s][ts=786s][ts=839s][ts=846s]. It replaces that mindset with a three-step data-driven pipeline: collect labeled images, train a classifier from examples, and evaluate on unseen images with a predict function [ts=896s][ts=947s][ts=970s][ts=983s]. The first concrete classifier is nearest neighbor: training simply memorizes all labeled examples [ts=1051s][ts=1057s], while prediction finds the most similar training image and copies its label [ts=1070s][ts=1088s][ts=1092s]. Similarity is defined with L1 distance, the sum of absolute pixel-wise differences between two images [ts=1149s][ts=1154s][ts=1178s]. The implementation is conceptually simple, but the complexity profile is poor for deployment: train is O(1) because it stores data, whereas predict is O(N) per test example because it compares against every stored image [ts=1416s][ts=1420s][ts=1457s][ts=1534s].

key_points.text: The data-driven recipe is: collect labeled data, train a classifier, then predict on new images.
Nearest neighbor training memorizes all examples and labels.
Nearest neighbor prediction returns the label of the most similar training image.
L1 distance is the sum of absolute pixel-wise differences between two images.
Nearest neighbor has O(1) training time and O(N) prediction time per test example.

kp_catalog.name: Data-driven supervised learning pipeline
kp_catalog.description: Pre-deep-learning progress came from shifting from hand-coded rules to learnable statistical models trained from data. The collect-train-predict paradigm: gather labeled data, fit a model, and evaluate predictions on unseen examples.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because data-driven supervised learning pipeline formalizes how pre-deep-learning progress came from shifting from hand-coded rules to learnable statistical models trained from data, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context while roles split between gateway and supporting and importance labels split between critical and high.

kp_catalog.name: Nearest neighbor classification
kp_catalog.description: A nonparametric classifier that stores all labeled examples and predicts by copying the label of the closest stored example.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because nearest neighbor classification clarifies a nonparametric classifier that stores all labeled examples and predicts by copying the label of the closest stored example, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Distance metrics for similarity
kp_catalog.description: The choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because distance metrics for similarity clarifies the choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
